summary: David organizes the gatekeepers, military divisions, and officials of his kingdom, then summons the assembly to charge Solomon with building the temple — revealing divinely given plans — before offering a great doxology and prayer as the people give generously; Psalm 127 (a Song of Ascents by Solomon) frames all human endeavor within God's sovereign provision.
psalm_rows_html:
TypeAuthorSituationKey ThemeNotable VerseNT Connection
Wisdom / Song of AscentsSolomonA pilgrimage song reflecting on God's sovereignty over all human work, family, and city lifeUnless the LORD builds the house and watches the city, all human labor is vain; children are a heritage and gift from the LORD"Unless the LORD builds the house, those who build it labor in vain." (v.1)Christ as the cornerstone of the Church, the true house God builds (Eph 2:20-22; 1 Cor 3:7 — "neither he who plants nor he who waters is anything, but only God who gives the growth")
